assertions.expectation: Explains that clusters on Regular channel will be force-upgraded to the next minor version (1.31) when 1.30 hits EoS
assertions.reason: Clearly states that Regular channel clusters will have control plane and node pools automatically upgrade to 1.31 when EoS is reached

assertions.expectation: Explains that clusters on Extended channel get up to 24 months of support, so 1.30 EoS is significantly later — they have more time
assertions.reason: Explicitly mentions 24-month support period for Extended channel, with standard support until April 2025 and extended support until April 2026

assertions.expectation: Explains that legacy 'No channel' clusters: control plane EoS minor versions are auto-upgraded to next supported minor, AND EoS node pools are force-upgraded even when 'no auto-upgrade' is configured
assertions.reason: Specifically states that legacy clusters have 'systematic enforcement' for control plane and 'node pool enforcement' for all node pools regardless of auto-upgrade settings

assertions.expectation: Mentions that a 'no upgrades' maintenance exclusion (30 days) can temporarily defer even EoS force-upgrades
assertions.reason: Explicitly mentions that only the 30-day 'no upgrades' exclusion can temporarily defer (but not prevent) enforcement

assertions.expectation: Recommends proactive upgrade planning rather than waiting for EoS enforcement
assertions.reason: Includes 'Proactive Upgrade Strategy' section with Option A to 'Stay ahead of EoS' and upgrade to 1.31 before April 2025

assertions.expectation: Mentions GKE EoS notifications via Cloud Logging as a planning tool
assertions.reason: Does not mention Cloud Logging notifications for EoS - only mentions checking upgrade info via gcloud commands

assertions.expectation: Mentions the deprecation insights dashboard for checking API compatibility before the forced upgrade
assertions.reason: Includes 'Set up monitoring for deprecation insights' in the recommended action plan

assertions.expectation: Warns that 'snowflaking' (manually freezing versions) should be avoided — it creates security and reliability risks
assertions.reason: Does not mention 'snowflaking' or warn against manually freezing versions - focuses on channel migration and upgrade strategies but doesn't address the security/reliability risks of avoiding upgrades
